name: Speaker Event Studio
description: Proposal template for planning medium-sized events with dozens of attendees, a few speakers, and a tighter run-of-show. Early idea only and meant to be customized.
author: ClawMax Team

communities.name: Speaker Event
communities.description: Shared coordination space for a speaker-driven event team.

groups.name: Program
groups.description: Agenda design, session flow, and attendee experience.

groups.name: Speakers
groups.description: Speaker outreach, prep, bios, assets, and communication.

groups.name: Logistics
groups.description: Venue, AV, seating, check-in, timing, and operational readiness.

groups.name: Follow-Up
groups.description: Post-event notes, thank-yous, recap, and next steps.

groups.name: Status
groups.description: Current priorities, dependencies, and launch readiness.

agents.name: Event Director
agents.role: Event lead — defines the event goal, final agenda shape, and owner decisions needed to ship the event cleanly.

agents.name: Speaker Coordinator
agents.role: Speaker specialist — manages outreach, confirmations, briefing, assets, and speaker logistics.

agents.name: Program Manager
agents.role: Program specialist — builds the agenda, pacing, and audience flow across sessions and transitions.

agents.name: Operations Manager
agents.role: Operations specialist — handles venue setup, AV, check-in, seating, and event-day execution.

agents.name: Follow Up Manager
agents.role: Follow-up specialist — captures outcomes, sends thanks, and turns the event into next-step actions.

workflows.name: Speaker Event Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the speaker event team and align on goals, format, and decision points.
workflows.content:
# Speaker Event Kickoff

You are the Event Director. The speaker event studio just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Event name and goal:** [launch, salon, meetup, community talk, customer event, custom]
- **Date, venue, and format:** [date, location, in-person, hybrid, custom]
- **Expected attendees:** [count or range]
- **Speaker count:** [planned speakers or panelists]
- **Key audience outcome:** [leads, learning, community, recruiting, custom]
- **Output artifact:** [run-of-show, speaker packet, ops plan, recap]

## Your Tasks
1. Restate the event goal, audience, venue, and timing
2. Ask Program Manager for the first agenda draft
3. Ask Speaker Coordinator for the speaker outreach and prep plan
4. Ask Operations Manager for the venue and check-in readiness plan
5. Set the initial event status and owner decisions in Status

workflows.name: Speaker Readiness Review
workflows.description: Check speaker confirmations, briefs, assets, and presentation readiness.
workflows.content:
# Speaker Readiness Review

1. Review speaker confirmations, bios, assets, and talk status
2. Identify who still needs briefing, timing guidance, or reminders
3. Flag missing slides, headshots, approvals, or travel details
4. Post the speaker readiness update to Speakers and any risks to Status

workflows.name: Run Of Show Build
workflows.description: Build the agenda and transitions needed for a smooth audience experience.
workflows.content:
# Run Of Show Build

1. Build the agenda with session timing, transitions, and owner handoffs
2. Balance speaker time, attendee energy, and buffer windows
3. Call out the top pacing, sequencing, or content risks
4. Post the run-of-show draft to Program and the key dependencies to Status

workflows.name: Event Day Ops Check
workflows.description: Finalize venue, AV, staffing, and check-in readiness before launch.
workflows.content:
# Event Day Ops Check

1. Review venue readiness, AV, seating, signage, and check-in flow
2. Confirm staffing, volunteer, or host responsibilities
3. Identify the last operational blockers and contingency plans
4. Post the event-day ops plan to Logistics and a go/no-go summary to Status

workflows.name: Speaker Event Follow Up
workflows.description: Summarize the event, capture next steps, and send the follow-up plan.
workflows.content:
# Speaker Event Follow Up

1. Summarize what happened, what worked, and what needs action next
2. Draft thank-you, recap, or attendee follow-up messages
3. Capture leads, commitments, and outstanding owner actions
4. Post the follow-up plan to Follow-Up and the executive summary to Status
